name: Cannoli (Cream Rolls)
recipeInstructions:
For the Shell:
Sift together 3 cups Flour, 1/4 cup Sugar, 1 tsp Ground cinnamon, and 1/4 tsp Salt into a bowl. Cut in 1/4 cup Shortening until the mixture resembles small peas. Stir in 2 beaten Eggs. Blend in 2 tbsp White vinegar and 2 tbsp Cold water. Knead dough on a lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic (about 5-8 minutes). Wrap in waxed paper and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
For the Filling:
Combine Ricotta cheese, Sugar, Vanilla extract, Finely chopped Candied Citron, and Semi-sweet chocolate pieces. Beat with an electric mixer for about 10 minutes until smooth. Stir in the chocolate pieces and let chill in the refrigerator.
Assembly:
Roll chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to about 1/8 inch thickness. Cut into rectangles using pastry cutters. Place each rectangle around a prepared cannoli tube, sealing edges with beaten Egg white. Blanch Pistachio nuts by boiling briefly, then cool and chop finely.
Frying:
Heat oil in a deep saucepan or automatic deep-fryer to 360°F (180°C). Fry the shells until golden brown (about 8 minutes), turning occasionally for even cooking. Drain on paper towels and let cool slightly before removing from tubes.
Serving:
Fill each cooled cannoli shell with chilled Ricotta filling. Sprinkle chopped nuts over the tops and dust with confectioners' sugar. Yield: About 16-18 cannoli.
